name: finish-the-projection-model-20260408-1628
idea:
Finish the projection-model rollback that a previous megaplan started but only got ~60% done. Currently the data shape is soft-tag (pinnedShotGroups[i] = { shotId, trackId, clipIds }) but several production code paths still operate on the legacy projection-model fields (start, children, offset, duration) behind compatibility shims that are silently lossy. The result: every interaction with a shot group goes through a hybrid where live preview uses soft-tag overrides but commit goes through legacy materialize paths, causing rounding drift, position jumps, stuck preview frames after release, and pointer-capture-loss races.

This is a NARROW, SCOPED finish-the-job megaplan, not a re-architecture. We know exactly what's left.

State of partial completion:

DONE (don't touch unless broken):
- PinnedShotGroup type is soft-tag in src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts (with deprecated start? and children? legacy compat fields that should be DELETED at the end of this refactor)
- pinned-group-projection.ts collapsed to ~280 lines: findEnclosingPinnedGroup, categorizeSelection, orderClipIdsByAt (real) + buildGroupFromChildren, buildGroupFromConfigClips, replaceGroupChildren, moveGroup, projectPinnedShotGroups (deprecated compatibility shims that should be DELETED at the end of this refactor)
- useClipDrag.ts rewritten for soft-tag model: uses enclosingGroup.group.clipIds, computes group bounds from live actions, uses orderClipIdsByAt to re-derive clip order after drags
- useClipResize.ts rewritten with resolveGroupMembers, getInteriorNeighbor, computeClipResizeMetaPatch, applyActionBounds helpers — interior-boundary commit branch is in place but routes through applyEdit({type: 'rows'}) which goes through the lossy materializeData path
- useShotGroups.ts rewritten to derive children/start from live row actions
- interaction-state.ts shared interaction lib created with createInteractionState, isInteractionActive, onInteractionEnd, notifyInteractionEndIfIdle
- useTimelinePersistence.ts save scheduler IS gated on interactionStateRef and flushes on gesture end
- usePollSync.ts poll-acceptance gate IS gated on interactionStateRef and re-evaluates on gesture end
- interactionStateRef is plumbed through VideoEditorProvider data context
- TimelineCanvas resize session model collapsed to one discriminated union: 'clip' | 'group-child-boundary' | 'group-edge' with one handleResizePointerDown, updateResize, endResize, handleResizePointerMove

NOT DONE (this megaplan):

1. useClipResize.ts interior-boundary commit drift. The interior commit calls applyEdit({type: 'rows', rows: nextRows, metaUpdates}). applyEdit for 'rows' mutations routes through materializeData -> rowsToConfig -> assembleTimelineData -> configToRows. rowsToConfig rounds clip positions to 2 decimal places (0.01s precision) and serializes hold clips as (at, hold) rounded INDEPENDENTLY, which means derived_end != round(actual_end) and tiny gaps can open between adjacent clips after every commit. Fix by increasing rowsToConfig rounding precision to at least 4 decimal places, OR better, make hold clip at/end serialization consistent.

2. usePinnedGroupSync rewrite for soft-tag model in src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usePinnedShotGroups.ts. Currently iterates projection-model children/offset/duration arrays. Rewrite to watch underlying Shot.images[] for added/removed/reordered generation_ids and rebuild clipIds accordingly, deferring during interaction.

3. useSwitchToFinalVideo.ts rewrite for soft-tag model. Currently builds projection-shape nextChildren arrays and passes to buildGroupFromChildren/replaceGroupChildren shims. Rewrite to compute the final-video clip's at/hold directly, update pinnedShotGroups[i].clipIds to [finalVideoClipId], save image clip positions in imageClipSnapshot. Restore reads imageClipSnapshot and restores row actions with updated clipIds.

4. useExternalDrop.ts rewrite for soft-tag shot drop. Compute clip positions directly when adding row actions, create soft-tag group with clipIds.

5. useTimelineTrackManagement.ts moveGroup callsites. Currently uses moveGroup shim which only updates trackId. Rewrite three callsites to also re-derive each clip's row assignment in meta.

6. Migration in migrate.ts repairConfig. Add one-way migration: when loading a saved config with projection-shape groups, convert to soft-tag by extracting clipIds = children.map((c) => c.clipId) and dropping start/children. Add unit test.

7. Delete deprecated compatibility shims from pinned-group-projection.ts: buildGroupFromChildren, buildGroupFromConfigClips, replaceGroupChildren, moveGroup, projectPinnedShotGroups. Also delete from PinnedShotGroup type: deprecated start? and children? fields. Verify type-check clean.

8. Update or delete the 14 failing tests in: serialize.test.ts (1), timeline-save-utils.test.ts (3), useClipDrag.test.tsx (3), useClipEditing.test.ts (6), useShotGroups.test.ts (1). Rewrite to test soft-tag model or delete if invariant no longer makes sense.

9. Remove diagnostic console.logs for tags: [handleResizePointerDown], [outer-edge lookup], [GroupEdgeResize], [startGroupEdgeResize], [useClipDrag], [Marquee].

Hard requirements:
1. Two user-reported symptoms must be fixed: boundary jumps closer to adjacent clip / cuts into other side; doesn't release frames after release.
2. All listed flows must continue to work end-to-end (drag clip in group, drag edges, interior boundary, cross-track, switch to final video, switch to images, gallery drop, auto-save defer, poll-sync defer, projection-shape load migration, shot label menu, per-clip context menu lockdown).
3. tsc --noEmit clean for video-editor tree.
4. All video-editor tests pass.
5. The 5 deprecated shims and 2 legacy fields physically deleted.
6. No diagnostic console.logs left.

Constraints: scope is video-editor tool only. Follow CLAUDE.md. Prefer Edit over Write. Don't bundle unrelated cleanups. Pre-existing failing tests and TS errors in unrelated files left alone. Don't introduce new state management libraries.

Success criteria:
1. Two user-reported symptoms no longer reproduce.
2. tsc --noEmit clean for video-editor tree.
3. All video-editor tests pass.
4. Deprecated shims and legacy PinnedShotGroup fields deleted.
5. No diagnostic console.logs left.
6. Loading saved file with projection-shape pinnedShotGroups works (migration test passes).
7. Code line count for shot-group-related files decreases net.
